steps.label: cause
steps.text: Juice is bled from a red-wine fermentation after a period of contact with dark grape skins.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Skin contact before bleeding extracts pigment, flavour and some phenolic material into the juice.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Compared with a pale blended rose, the wine can show deeper colour, riper fruit and more phenolic structure.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: saignee derives colour and structure from red-grape skin contact before the juice is separated.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
